Analyzes static memory usage of ELF files with options for verbosity, summary, and speed.

# Purpose
This script analyzes ELF (Executable and Linkable Format) files using the `analyze_elf` function from the `binutils` module. It uses the `argparse` library to handle command-line arguments, allowing users to specify options such as `--verbose`, `--summary`, `--fast`, and `--sections`, as well as the ELF file to analyze. The script processes the specified ELF file and retrieves its sections based on the provided options. It then iterates over the sections and prints their details using the `pprint` method, with the output format controlled by the `--summary` and `--verbose` flags.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `argparse`
- `binutils.analyze_elf`
